2606.20526.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
2606.20526.paper_summary: Neurosymbolic systems such as DeepProbLog combine neural perception with probabilistic logic, but standard inference is associational. Counterfactual reasoning additionally requires a causal semantics for interventions and evidence. We introduce DeepSWIP, a single-world counterfactual semantics for DeepProbLog programs. Using neural materialization, we reduce fixed-context neural predicates to ordinary ProbLog choices, apply Single World Intervention Programs (SWIPs), and compute counterfactuals by weighted model counting (WMC) over a single transformed program. Under finite grounding and unique-supported-model assumptions, DeepSWIP is exact relative to the learned materialized FCM. The standard quotient-WMC form of ProbLog conditionals identifies active neural probabilities and explains intervention cleaning, calibration sensitivity, and rare-evidence instability. Experiments on MPI3D confirm the transformation against a DeepTwin construction against 12,000 queries, as predicted and a 2.14$\times$ inference speedup from avoiding the Twin's endogenous duplication. A SUMO HOV experiment shows that neural calibration degradation biases plug-in estimates, while a correctly scoped randomized-policy AIPW estimator removes most first-order bias for population mean and ATE estimands. Code is at https://github.com/saibib/deep_SWIP.
2606.20526.paper_summary_zh: 神經符號系統如 DeepProbLog 將神經感知與概率邏輯結合，但標準推理是關聯性的。反事實推理還需要對干預和證據的因果語義。我們介紹了 DeepSWIP，這是一種針對 DeepProbLog 程式的單世界反事實語義。通過神經具現化，我們將固定上下文的神經謂詞簡化為普通的 ProbLog 選擇，應用單世界干預程序（SWIPs），並通過加權模型計數（WMC）在單一轉換程序上計算反事實。在有限基礎和唯一支持模型的假設下，DeepSWIP 相對於學習到的具現化 FCM 是精確的。ProbLog 條件的標準商 WMC 形式識別主動的神經概率，並解釋干預清理、校準敏感性和稀有證據不穩定性。對 MPI3D 的實驗確認了與 DeepTwin 結構的轉換，針對 12,000 個查詢，預測的結果以及避免 Twin 的內生重複所帶來的 2.14$\times$ 推理加速。一個 SUMO HOV 實驗顯示，神經校準退化會使插件估計產生偏差，而正確範疇的隨機政策 AIPW 估計器則消除了大部分對於人口均值和 ATE 估計量的一階偏差。代碼位於 https://github.com/saibib/deep_SWIP。
2606.20526.author: Saimun Habib et.al.
2606.20526.authors: Saimun Habib, Vaishak Belle, Fengxiang He
